definition: A phospholipid bilayer that separates the interior of the cell from the outside environment and regulates what passes through
A network of membranes involved in protein and lipid synthesis; comes in rough (with ribosomes) and smooth forms
Organelle that modifies proteins from the ER and packages them into vesicles for transport or secretion
A single-celled organism (bacteria or archaea) with no membrane-bound nucleus or organelles
An organism whose cells contain a membrane-bound nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles
Adenosine triphosphate — the primary energy currency of the cell
The metabolic pathway that converts glucose and oxygen into ATP
A stage of aerobic respiration in the mitochondrial matrix that oxidizes acetyl-CoA to CO2 and generates NADH and FADH2
